text.title: Fine-tune vs prompt decision tree — worked example
text.summary: A worked decision where routing beats fine-tune for a chat-classifier workload.

section.title: Context
section.p: Workload: incoming-message classifier (8 labels). 80k req/day. p95 latency 320ms. Current $/req 1.4c. F1 0.79 vs target 0.85.

section.title: Axis scores

list.item: quality = 3 (F1 below target but not catastrophic).

section.p: Two axes scored ≤ 2 (cost only at "below target"). Quality at 3 is borderline.

list.item: RAG: +0.0 F1 (labels are content-bound, retrieval helps little).
routing (Haiku for high-confidence, Sonnet for low): +0.04 F1, $/req drops to 0.7c.

section.title: Decision
section.p: Routing alone closes quality gap (+0.04 ≥ +0.06 needed? close) AND halves cost. Fine-tune is uncertain because: only one axis below target after routing, training spend $1500, ops overhead persistent. Recommendation: `routing` now, revisit at triggers.

section.title: Final record
